marketState: neutral-to-bearish, but crowded short positioning caps conviction
summary: Front-month NG is trading at $3.196, slightly above the modeled fair value of $2.929 (band $2.429-$3.429), and fell about 1.5% from the prior close of $3.245. Seasonality now shows a weak but bearish-leaning edge (Low reliability): only 19% odds of being higher in 4 weeks and a typical 4-week move of -1.8%, even though YTD performance (-6.2%) is fractionally better than the seasonal median (-7.1%). Storage is running a +175 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average with a solid +87 Bcf weekly build, a bearish supply backdrop into the shoulder season. Weather cooling demand is running +15% above the 5-year normal but has dropped sharply day-over-day (-19.2) and now sits at the low end of its 7-day range; the 1-7D and 8-16D demand forecast fields needed to confirm the near-term trajectory are missing from this snapshot, which is the single biggest gap in the picture. Managed money is already net short at only the 18th percentile of its 5-year range, so while the broader data leans bearish, the crowded short book raises squeeze risk and argues for smaller size and patience rather than chasing the move.

longTerm.entry: $3.20-$3.30 (scale in on any bounce toward fair-value band upper half)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% risk (reduced size given crowded MM short)
longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y, still building), a bearish seasonal skew (only 19% odds higher in 4wks, typical move -1.8%), and price already slightly over fair value ($3.196 vs $2.929 fair) all argue for fading strength. The weekly technical trend is also labeled bearish with a 50<200 EMA cross.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close back above the fair-value band top (~$3.43) or a storage report that flips to a deficit/undersized build would invalidate the bearish thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: The 1-7D and 8-16D weather demand fields are both null, removing the most important short-term input. Technicals are mixed (5m overbought above the upper Bollinger band vs. bearish 50<200 EMA crosses on 15m/1h/1d) and the futures curve object is missing, so there is no clean directional edge for a 1-10 day trade right now.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for the 1-7D/8-16D weather demand data to populate, or for a decisive close above ~$3.35 (fair-value band upper half) to short, or below ~$3.10 (today's low area) to confirm downside continuation.
